programme.title: In the Americas With David Yetman
programme.sub-title: Imperiled Refuges: Islets in the Sea of Cortes
programme.desc: Islands and waters of Mexico's northern Gulf of California provide refuge for sea creatures reliant on fish stocks and beset with the uncertainties of climate change.

programme.title: Outside: Beyond the Lens
programme.desc: Jeff, Zack and Dave, along with local guides, discover the history and culture of Hvar's past.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Rebecca Hall and Lee Daniels research their family histories.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: General Howe takes Philadelphia, but the Americans' surprising victory over General Burgoyne at Saratoga encourages France to support the U.S.

programme.title: Lafayette: The Lost Hero
programme.desc: Marquis de Lafayette left France at the age of 19 and helped fight for America's freedom.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: America's Home Cooking: Flavors of the Fourth
programme.desc: Celebrating picnics and barbecue with Chris Fennimore and the America's Home Cooking team; recipes include horseradish potato salad, baked beans, apple hand pies and more summer favorites.

programme.title: Rick Steves' Europe: Great German Cities
programme.desc: Rick Steves explores the German cities of Hamburg, Dresden, Leipzig, Frankfurt and Nuremberg, including their Baroque palaces, skyscrapers, riversides and beer halls.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.sub-title: The Artist Formerly Known as Liana; Jamaica Day
programme.desc: Liana accidentally submits art that Stu made and feels guilty taking the credit for his work; Lyla and Luke perform an "Anansi" folktale puppet show.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: The Kratt Bros. discover there is an old wily orca who only eats large sharks; they find this hard to believe, so with the help of Aviva's dolphin decoder, modified to speak orca, the Wild Kratts go in search of the orca who likes sharks.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Arthur's not sure what to make of the new baby; D.W. considers running away to Button Island.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: The Hunters find a stowaway in their picnic basket, a little furry dog.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.sub-title: The Artist Formerly Known as Liana; Jamaica Day
programme.desc: Liana accidentally submits art that Stu made and feels guilty taking the credit for his work; Lyla and Luke perform an "Anansi" folktale puppet show.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
programme.desc: Carl surprises Sheldon with a lemonade stand, but is confused when Sheldon runs off; Carl's caterpillars turn into butterflies.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.sub-title: Daniel's Blueberry Paws; Wow at the Library
programme.desc: Daniel Tiger's treat from Prince Tuesday's fruity ice treat cart starts to melt when he becomes distracted by all the other flavors.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Jay and Lupe use wayfinding signs to track down Phoebe; Pete and Craig face an epic hoops battle on the courts.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: When Oscar tries to find someone disappointed by his disappoint-o-meter, he accidentally makes them happy.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.desc: When Bex removes her cochlear implant and hearing aid so she can go on the waterslide, Sammy and the Wombats find new ways to communicate; the Wombats love Leiko's new mural.

programme.title: Donkey Hodie
programme.desc: Donkey Hodie and pals help Purple Panda celebrate his first Halloween in Someplace Else.

programme.title: Rosie's Rules
programme.sub-title: Rosie the Reporter; The Jalapeno Giant
programme.desc: Rosie tries to be a reporter but has trouble finding things that are newsworthy; Rosie and Jun believe there is a jalapeno giant on the loose.

programme.title: Elinor Wonders Why
programme.sub-title: Mother's Day; Tricks of the Jade
programme.desc: Elinor and her dad learn how to make their own chocolate; after accidentally breaking a jade plant, the kids learn how the plant grows.

programme.title: Alma's Way
programme.sub-title: Rubberiest Rubber Bands; Donuts for Doggies
programme.desc: Alma and Rafia try to give away rubber bands; Alma and Rafia think they've invited too many people to Safina's bake sale.

programme.sub-title: Froggy of Denali; Molly Mabray and the Mystery Stones
programme.desc: Molly wants to keep a frog as a pet -- until she realizes it requires high-maintenance; Molly and Tooey can't wait to see the petroglyphs at the beach.

programme.sub-title: I Am Gwen Ifill; I Am Matthew Henson

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Rebecca Hall and Lee Daniels research their family histories.

programme.title: Our Vanishing Americana: Florida
programme.desc: Our Vanishing Florida Americana showcases photographer Mike Lassiter's quest to capture the essence of Florida's back roads, coastlines, and main streets.

programme.title: MacGillivray Freeman's Coral Reef Adventure
programme.desc: Filmmakers Howard and Michele Hall embark on a daring expedition across the South Pacific to document the world's most beautiful and endangered coral reefs.

programme.title: BBC News The Context
programme.desc: A regular panel of guests from across the political spectrum discuss the biggest international stories of the day.

programme.title: BBC News America
programme.desc: Live news program produced out of BBC's Washington, D.C. bureau; host Sumi Somaskanda.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Energy Switch
programme.desc: Politicians from different parties collaborate on climate and energy challenges; with Kay Bailey Hutchison and Joe Manchin.

programme.title: American Grown: My Job Depends on Ag
programme.desc: The future of the agriculture industry and stories of how it touches us all far beyond the table.

programme.title: Nature
programme.desc: Exploring the unique evolutionary journey of woodpeckers and the role they play in every ecosystem, except Antarctica and Australia.

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: Archaeological investigations into the origins of democracy in Greece 2,500 years ago; mass graves and ancient ruins where tyrants were murdered shine light on the birth of a revolutionary form of government.

programme.title: Shared Planet
programme.desc: From New York to Kolkata, dicovering the surprising and beneficial ways urban wildlife transforms cities into thriving, shared ecosystems.

programme.title: Human Footprint
programme.desc: Exploring the history and science of cotton.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: Jupiter's gravitational force made it a wrecking ball as it barreled through the early solar system, but it also helped shape life on Earth as it brought comets laden with water and possibly the asteroid that put an end to the dinosaurs.

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: NASA's Cassini probe explores Saturn's icy rings and moons, capturing ring-moon interactions and revealing ingredients for life on the moon Enceladus.

programme.title: Aging Backwards 4: The Miracle of Flexibility With Miranda Esmonde-White
programme.desc: Gentle flexibility and range of motion exercises can improve posture and help reverse the signs of aging, improve organ health and increase energy.

programme.sub-title: The Artist Formerly Known as Liana; Jamaica Day
programme.desc: Liana accidentally submits art that Stu made and feels guilty taking the credit for his work; Lyla and Luke perform an "Anansi" folktale puppet show.

programme.desc: The Kratt brothers use a new archery skill to rescue baby animals from Donita Donata's yacht.

programme.desc: The Hunters search for a way to monitor the weather after they lose power during a thunderstorm.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: The Hunters search for a way to monitor the weather after they lose power during a thunderstorm.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood

programme.desc: Donkey and Panda have different ideas about how to celebrate Best Friends Day; while getting ready to make big art, the pals get stuck to a big glue bottle.

programme.title: Rosie's Rules
programme.sub-title: Lights Out Rosie; Rosie Goes Camping
programme.desc: Rosie and Javi learn how people did things before there was electricity after a storm causes a power outage; Rosie learns to use tools from nature during a family camping trip.

programme.title: Elinor Wonders Why
programme.sub-title: Melting Point; Mr. Raccoon's Bad Day
programme.desc: While delivering chocolates, the kids learn that warmer temperatures can melt treats; the kids help Mr. Raccoon calm down when he is having a bad day.

programme.title: Alma's Way
programme.sub-title: Alma Goes to Puerto Rico; Bomba on the Beach
programme.desc: When Alma and her family travel to Puerto Rico to visit Papi's family, Alma can't wait to dance Bomba on the beach; her dancing plans are put on hold when an unmarked package containing a beautiful Bomba skirt is dropped in front of her bisabuela.

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.sub-title: Sea Lion Crooks and Halibut Hooks; Nature's Medicine
programme.desc: While fishing, Molly and her cousin, Randall, encounter a sea lion with a strange word written on its back; Molly's scout troop halts its hike when Trini scrapes her knee.

programme.title: Human Footprint
programme.desc: Exploring the modern city, including three species that thrive in that ecosystem.

programme.title: Life Unearthed With Ariel Waldman
programme.desc: Following Ariel Waldman's solo expedition through Antarctica's extreme environments as she documents the thriving microscopic wildlife beneath the ice.

programme.title: Life Unearthed With Ariel Waldman
programme.desc: Set in Antarctica's Dry Valleys, Antarctica's glacial and icy ecosystems reveal how frozen landscapes can sustain surprising life forms.

programme.title: Julie Taboulie's Lebanese Kitchen
programme.desc: Tabouli; lamb kebabs; Lebanese-style french fries; a garlic, olive oil and lemon sauce called toum.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When his priceless Blue Cross is targeted by the infamous criminal Flambeau, Father Brown takes the initiative to try and save his soul.
